name: Product promise
summary: Prism is a private-by-default, creative-by-design AI workspace for calm, multi-perspective thinking.
details: Prism gives people one controlled place to think, create, and experiment with AI while keeping routine and sensitive work local. Cloud models are available when they earn their place, but provider choice and behavior remain visible to the user.

name: LOCAL mode privacy boundary
summary: The LOCAL/ONLINE toggle is a hard privacy boundary: LOCAL turns must not silently leave the user's machine.
details: Provider selection, auxiliary model work, embeddings, and image generation all need to preserve the visible LOCAL promise. External calls are allowed only through explicit online/provider paths or configured local-network services such as Ollama and Qdrant.

name: Experience lanes
summary: Chat, Zen, Coffee, Sandbox, and Story are distinct lanes with different emotional weight and control surfaces.
details: Chat is the continuity companion lane, Zen is calmer 1:1 interaction, Coffee is multi-bot conversation, Sandbox is the runtime-control lane, and Story is the emerging playable narrative lane. The split protects both practical control and experiential clarity.

name: Free desktop distribution
summary: Official Prism desktop builds are free; optional Patreon support is patronage, not product access.
details: The active distribution model is direct desktop releases for macOS, Windows, and Linux, plus an iPhone PWA path served by Prism. Support should remain quiet, optional, non-gating, and separate from core functionality.

name: Memory and continuity pipeline
summary: Prism stores scoped conversation state, encrypted memory records, and summary/search context while respecting incognito boundaries.
details: The backend keeps users, sessions, conversations, messages, bots, memories, memory summaries, images, and exports scoped by user. Incognito conversations avoid memory reads and writes. Semantic memory uses local embedding paths rather than hidden cloud fallback.

name: Monorepo architecture
summary: Prism is a Node.js, Next.js, Electron, SQLite, Qdrant, and Ollama workspace organized as apps plus shared packages.
details: The API owns local data, auth, providers, memory, and orchestration. The web app owns the product interface. The desktop app wraps and stages the runtime. Shared packages hold cross-surface contracts and model-routing helpers.

name: LOCAL mode privacy boundary
summary: LOCAL mode is a hard privacy guarantee, not a preference hint.
details: PRISM routes local turns through Ollama-backed providers and pins this behavior with provider tests so automatic escalation cannot leak work to cloud providers.

name: Experience lanes
summary: Chat, Zen, Coffee, Story, and Sandbox serve distinct user experiences instead of one overloaded chat surface.
details: PRISM keeps practical controls and experiential presence in separate lanes so each mode can stay clear, calm, and purpose-built.

name: Experience lanes
summary: Chat, Zen, Coffee, Story, and Sandbox serve distinct user experiences instead of one overloaded chat surface.
details: PRISM keeps practical controls and experiential presence in separate lanes so each mode can stay clear, calm, and purpose-built.

name: Bot library groups and marketplace installs
summary: The Chat and Sandbox bot canvas can organize bots into saved groups and install marketplace bots, packs, starter bots, and Lenses with explicit progress feedback.
details: Custom bot groups are mutable saved views with capacity limits, dashboard cards, delete protection, and context-menu maintenance. A single selected bot can be added to an existing custom group, focused group cards return to the full bot directory when the user clicks outside the card, and group detail dialogs close only through visible controls so text selection does not dismiss them. Marketplace bot, theme, starter-pack, and Lens installs reuse the bot import overlay so install progress is visible while bot profiles and memories are restored.
